summary: My grandfather felt a strong calling to preach in the Methodist Church after he stopped farming. He served in various places across Oklahoma and even in Old Dalhart, Texas, preaching in about seven different churches during his career. I only remember hearing him preach once as a child. Being a Methodist minister back then meant very little pay, mostly just chickens, bread, and potatoes, so it was a tough life for him and our family.

Dalhart, Texas, was one of the places where my grandfather preached while serving as a Methodist minister. Although I was very young, I know he spent time there along with various locations in Oklahoma. It was part of his journey as he moved between churches, trying to support his family despite the meager pay. Dalhart was just one stop in a series of places tied to his ministry work.

Oklahoma was a big part of my early life and my grandfather's ministry. He preached in western, southern, and northern Oklahoma, moving between about seven different churches over time. After he sold our family farm due to financial troubles, we became nomads within Oklahoma, moving several times to places where we didn't have to pay rent. I started school in Oklahoma at the Riverdale School after we moved across the river to the Bates Place.

After a difficult period of moving around in Oklahoma, including living in a boxcar by the river, we eventually settled in Pond Creek, Oklahoma. This move came after about a year and a half of instability following the sale of our family farm. Pond Creek marked a more stable chapter in my childhood, especially as I began attending school regularly. It was a significant place for us after the hardships we faced.
